heading: Expense of weighing and measuring
label: 1494 Expense of weighing and measuring
text: In all cases in which the invoice or entry does not state the weight, quantity, or measure of the merchandise, the expense of ascertaining the same shall be collected from the importer of record before its release from customs custody.
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in , . That section was superseded by section 494 of act , comprising this section, and repealed by section 651(a)(1) of the 1930 act.act Sept. 21, 1922, ch. 356, title IV, § 49442 Stat. 964June 17, 1930A prior provision requiring merchandise to be weighed, gauged or measured at the expense of the owner, agent or consignee, in cases in which the invoice or entry did not contain the weight, quantity or measure was contained in R.S. § 2920, prior to repeal by , .act Sept. 21, 1922, ch. 356, title IV, § 64242 Stat. 989
Amendments1983— substituted “importer of record” for “consignee” after “collected from the”.Pub. L. 97–446
